sections.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty -sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, amendments were made in the Annex.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
1.1 This standard applies to sorghum flour destined for direct human consumption as defined in Section 2.1 below.
1.2 This standard does not apply to grits or meal obtained from Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench.

1.1 This standard applies to sorghum flour destined for direct human consumption as defined in Section 2.1 below.
1.2 This standard does not apply to grits or meal obtained from Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Sorghum flour is the product obtained from grains of Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench through a process of industrial milling during which the seed coat is removed and the germ is removed to a large extent and the endosperm is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION
number=2
level=1
Sorghum flour is the product obtained from grains of Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench through a process of industrial milling during which the seed coat is removed and the germ is removed to a large extent and the endosperm is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness.

page_spans.text: Sorghum flour is the product obtained from grains of Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench through a process of industrial milling during which the seed coat is removed and the germ is removed to a large extent and the endosperm is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969),1 and other codes of practice recommended by the CAC which are relevant to this product.
5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product: 
– shall be free from micro-organisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health; 
– shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and 
– shall not contain any substance originating from micro-organisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health.

page_spans.text:
5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969),1 and other codes of practice recommended by the CAC which are relevant to this product.
5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product: 
– shall be free from micro-organisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health; 
– shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and 
– shall not contain any substance originating from micro-organisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text:
6.1 Sorghum flour shall be packaged in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

sections.title: Quality factors – general
sections.text:
3.1.1 Sorghum flour shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
3.1.2 Sorghum flour shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
3.1.3 Sorghum flour shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.

sections.title: METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended methods of analysis and sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard shall be used.

sections.text: The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “sorghum flour”.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: Information for non -retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

classification.function: Compliance obligation with official mycotoxin limits
classification.pattern: Declarative obligation referencing external mycotoxin limits

components.requirement: shall comply with maximum mycotoxin limits established by CAC

classification.justification: Similar structure and function to previous sentence; obligation to comply with specified limits; no template match.
notes: External compliance reference for mycotoxin limits.

sentences.original: Lower moisture limits should be required for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage.

classification.sentence: Lower moisture limits should be required for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage.
classification.function: Recommendation for Limits Based on Conditions
classification.pattern: Subject + should be required + Condition Clause

components.subject: Lower moisture limits
components.condition: for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage

classification.justification: Recommendation or obligation conditioned on factors; adapted template fit.
notes: Ambiguous whether obligation or recommendation but classified as Obligation due to 'should be required'.

sentences.original: The tannin content of sorghum flour shall not exceed 0.3 percent on a dry matter basis.

classification.sentence: The tannin content of sorghum flour shall not exceed 0.3 percent on a dry matter basis.
classification.pattern: Negative obligation defining a maximum quantitative threshold

components.limit: shall not exceed 0.3 percent on dry matter basis

classification.justification: Sets a maximum quantitative limit representing a regulatory threshold; no direct template match.
notes: Specific numerical limit expressed as negative obligation.

sentences.original: In addition to the requirements of the General standard for the labelling of pre -packaged foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

classification.sentence: In addition to the requirements of the General standard for the labelling of pre -packaged foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
classification.pattern: Introductory normative statement referring to general standard and adding specifics

components.reference_standard: General standard for the labelling of pre-packaged foods (CXS 1-1985)

classification.justification: Introductory normative statement signaling additional provisions; no matching template exists for such introductory normative context statements.
notes: Functions as a preamble to further obligations.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended methods of analysis and sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this Standard shall be used.
classification.pattern: Prepositional phrase + obligation declarative

components.Mandate: methods of analysis and sampling per Recommended Methods (CXS 234-1999)

classification.justification: Specification of mandatory methods for verification; obligation modality with structured reference; fits adapted template for compliance methods.

sentences.original: The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “sorghum flour”.

classification.sentence: The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “sorghum flour”.
classification.pattern: Declarative obligation specifying required label content

components.requirement: name of product to be shown on label

classification.justification: Direct obligation specifying mandatory label content; no known matching template.
notes: Simple obligation on labeling content.

sentences.original: Information for non -retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container.

classification.sentence: However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
classification.pattern: Subject + may be replaced + Condition Clause

components.subject: lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer
components.permission: may be replaced by an identification mark
components.condition: provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents

classification.justification: Permission modality with condition; partial match to permission templates.

sentences.original: In those instances where more than one factor limit and/or method of analysis is given, we strongly recommend that users specify the appropriate limit and method of analysis.

classification.sentence: In those instances where more than one factor limit and/or method of analysis is given, we strongly recommend that users specify the appropriate limit and method of analysis.
classification.pattern: Conditional clause + recommendation statement

components.condition: instances with more than one factor limit and/or method of analysis
components.recommendation: users specify the appropriate limit and method of analysis

classification.justification: Sentence expresses a strong recommendation conditional on multiple factors; no close match and structure is conditional plus recommendation (Permission modality).
notes: Recommendation rather than obligation

sentences.original: ASH Min: 0.9% on a dry matter basis – and – Max: 1.5% on a dry matter basis Refer to Section 8 PROTEIN (N x 6.25) Min: 8.5% on a dry matter basis CRUDE FAT Min: 2.2% on a dry matter basis– and – Max:4.7% on a dry matter basis CRUDE FIBRE Max: 1.8% on a dry matter basis

classification.sentence: Refer to section 8 COLOUR RANGE: 18 to 30 units Refer to Section 8 PARTICLE SIZE (GRANULARITY) Min: 100% of flour shall pass through a sieve the dimensions of the mesh being diameter of 0.5 mm for 1fine2 flour and a diameter of 1 mm for medium flour Refer to Section 8
classification.function: Specify compositional/physical criteria for product attributes and mandatory compliance requirement
classification.pattern: Reference to section, followed by attribute name and quantitative criteria, concluding with an obligation clause specifying mandatory passage through sieves with defined measurements
classification.justification: The sentence combines references, quantitative attribute range, and an obligation phrased as "shall pass", describing a complex multipart limit and compliance condition not aligned with existing templates.
notes: Mixed modality with limits and an explicit obligation clause
Structural complexity involves multiple attribute references with specific measurements

pages.title: STANDARD FOR SORGHUM FLOUR
pages.summary: A página contém o título principal do documento e informações de versão na seção de texto. Não há outras seções detectadas.
A página apresenta o cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 173-1989 2' e uma seção principal intitulada '2023 Amendments' com texto explicativo. Não há subdivisões nem continuidade entre páginas.

Página formal com hierarquia baseada em numeração decimal: seis seções principais (1 a 6) com subdivisões claras, texto associado aos títulos correspondentes e sem indicação de continuidade entre páginas. Cabeçalho repetitivo identificado.

A página contém as seções 7 e 8 do documento, com subseções 7.1 e 7.2 devidamente estruturadas e textos associados. O cabeçalho repetitivo foi identificado como 'CXS 173-1989 4'. Não há continuidade entre esta página e outras.

A página contém três seções principais: ANNEX, Factor/Description Limit Method of analysis e NOTES, todas sendo níveis 1, com seus respectivos textos associados. Não há subseções evidentes nesta página. O cabeçalho foi identificado e não há continuidade entre páginas.
